title: 10 best herbal teas for cough
intro_desc: Herbal teas for cough are infusions made from various plants, flowers, and spices, which can help alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds. These teas often contain natural anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties that can soothe irritated throats, reduce inflammation, and help expel mucus. Examples of herbal teas that help with cough include thyme, licorice root, slippery elm, and eucalyptus. By drinking these teas, individuals can find relief from coughing fits, leading to improved sleep, increased productivity, and an overall better quality of life. For instance, a study published in the Journal of Ethnopharmacology found that thyme tea was as effective as a common cough syrup in relieving acute bronchitis symptoms, highlighting the potential benefits of herbal teas for cough.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena teas helps with cough because of its natural soothing properties that ease irritation in the throat and chest. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce swelling and calm the cough reflex, providing relief from persistent hacking and tickling sensations. Additionally, lemon verbena's expectorant properties loosen and clear out mucus, allowing for easier breathing and a more restful night's sleep.
remedy_properties: Aromatic compounds: Lemon verbena contains aromatic compounds like citral, limonene, and linalool that help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with coughing.

Antitussive properties: The tea has been shown to have antitussive (cough-suppressing) effects due to its ability to reduce the frequency and intensity of coughing fits, making it a natural remedy for soothing irritated throats and calming coughs.

Antioxidant activity: Lemon verbena tea's high antioxidant content helps to protect the respiratory system from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to chronic coughing conditions like bronchitis and asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon verbena roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation in the throat and chest, relieving cough symptoms.
Bark: The bark of the lemon verbena plant contains flavonoids, which help soothe the mucous membranes and reduce congestion, providing relief from coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lemon verbena leaves measure about one tablespoon per cup of water.
Combine the lemon verbena leaves with boiling water in a heat-resistant glass teapot.
Steep the mixture for five to seven minutes or according to package instructions.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth.
Serve the tea hot or allow it to cool and refrigerate for later consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with cough because it soothes and calms the irritated mucous membranes in the throat, providing instant relief from scratchy throats and persistent coughing. The herbs in marshmallow tea, such as marshmallow root, licorice root, and slippery elm, work together to reduce inflammation and provide a protective barrier against irritants, allowing the body to heal naturally and expel excess mucus more effectively.
remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow tea contains a high amount of mucilage, a thick, gel-like substance that helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, making it easier to swallow and reducing coughing.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids and saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the throat and lungs, alleviating coughing and discomfort.
Expectorant properties: Marshmallow tea has natural expectorant properties that help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to cough up and reducing congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and calms irritated respiratory passages, making it effective for relieving coughs.
Bark: The bark contains saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the throat and chest, alleviating cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marshmallow roots, washing them thoroughly before drying or storing them in an airtight container for later use.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried marshmallow root or two teaspoons of fresh root material for every eight ounces of boiling water.

Combine the measured marshmallow root with the boiling water in a teapot or heat-resistant cup, allowing it to steep for 5-7 minutes or until the mixture reaches a comfortable temperature for consumption.
Strain the tea into a separate container using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding any solids and reserving the liquid for serving.
Serve the herbal marshmallow tea hot, sweetening it to taste with honey or sugar if desired, and drinking it while warm to fully appreciate its soothing properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Daisy teas helps with cough because of its natural soothing properties, which calm the irritated throat and loosen phlegm. The herbal blend contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the tea's expectorant qualities help bring up mucus from the lungs, providing relief from congestion and hacking coughs. As a result, sipping on a warm cup of daisy tea can provide fast and effective relief from persistent coughs.
remedy_properties: Expectorant Properties: Herbal daisy teas contain expectorants like calendula, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel the cough.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in herbal daisy teas, such as those found in chamomile, help to reduce inflammation in the throat and chest, which can alleviate discomfort and congestion associated with a cough.

Antioxidant Properties: Antioxidants like vitamin C and E in herbal daisy teas help to protect the body from oxidative stress caused by the coughing process, reducing damage to lung tissue and promoting recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, relieving congestion and coughing.
Bark: Contains tannins and other compounds that can help soothe an irritated throat and reduce inflammation, providing relief from coughing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried daisy flowers in late morning after dew has evaporated containing about one ounce.
Rinse the daisy flowers gently with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris and discard excess.
Combine the rinsed daisy flowers with boiling water in a teapot and steep for 5-7 minutes to release flavonoids and anthocyanins.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot removing solids and leaving liquid intact.
Enjoy the herbal daisy tea hot or chilled sweetened with honey if desired after 10-15 minutes of preparation time has elapsed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with cough because its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties soothe an irritated throat and reduce congestion. The flavonoids and carotenoids in the tea help to thin mucus, making it easier to expel, while its antibacterial properties combat infection-causing bacteria that can exacerbate a cough. This natural remedy also provides relief from bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory issues, promoting a healthy breathing system and alleviating discomfort associated with coughing.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold tea's antioxidants help to reduce inflammation in the throat and chest, which can alleviate cough symptoms caused by respiratory issues.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in pot marigold tea, such as lutein and zeaxanthin, work to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, reducing coughing fits and congestion.
Expectorant properties: Pot marigold tea's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of coughs.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in calcium and magnesium, roots are believed to help soothe a dry, irritated throat and reduce inflammation.
Flowers: Containing apigenin and luteolin, flowers have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate congestion and ease coughing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried calendula officinalis flowers from a trusted source or store-bought package in a clean glass jar.
Measure out one tablespoon of dried petals for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Bring the water to a boil in a medium-sized pot and pour it over the dried calendula petals.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the tea reaches your desired strength and color.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids and serving hot.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cannabis teas helps with cough because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the throat, reducing inflammation and irritation. The active ingredients in cannabis, such as THC and CBD, work together to relax the muscles in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and expel mucus. As a result, herbal cannabis teas provide fast relief from coughing fits, allowing individuals to rest comfortably without constant discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antitussive (cough-suppressing) compounds: Herbal cannabis teas contain a variety of terpenes and flavonoids that have been shown to suppress coughing, helping to relieve the uncomfortable and annoying symptoms of acute bronchitis, pneumonia, or other respiratory infections.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The anti-inflammatory properties of herbal cannabis teas can help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which is often a contributing factor to chronic coughs and other respiratory issues such as asthma, COPD, or bronchitis.

Expectorant properties: Many herbal cannabis tea blends contain expectorants like eucalyptus, thyme, and ginger that help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel phlegm and relieve congestion and coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: Cannabis roots have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in soothing a persistent cough.
Bark: The bark of the cannabis plant has expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, providing relief from a stubborn cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry high-quality cannabis sativa buds, leaves or flowers for 7-14 days to preserve potency.
Crush or grind 10-20 grams of dried cannabis material into small pieces to increase surface area.
Combine crushed cannabis with one quart of boiling water in a heat-resistant container and let steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a cheesecloth, fine-mesh sieve or nut milk bag to separate solids from liquids immediately after brewing.
Discard solids and store the herbal cannabis tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days before serving chilled or at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper teas helps with cough because it contains a unique combination of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that work together to soothe and calm the throat. The capsaicin present in bell peppers has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and congestion in the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing the urge to cough. Additionally, the natural sweetness of bell peppers helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing quick relief from a pesky cough.
remedy_properties: Thymol (found in Thyme tea): Has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated throats and airways.
Eucalyptol (found in Eucalyptus tea): Is a natural decongestant that helps to relieve respiratory congestion and ease coughs by opening up airways and reducing mucus production.
Catechins (found in Green Tea): Have antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of coughs, such as sore throats and congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the bell pepper plant contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe a sore throat and reduce coughing.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, or underground stems, have antimicrobial properties that combat infection and ease congestion caused by coughs.
remedy_recipe: Rinse fresh bell peppers carefully under cool running water removing any dirt or debris.
Chop 2-3 medium-sized bell peppers into small pieces and place them in a large glass jar.
Heat 2 cups of pure water to a simmering point and pour it over the chopped bell peppers.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes allowing the flavors and nutrients to infuse fully.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large teapot or individual cups.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cinnamon teas helps with cough because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the throat and relieve congestion. The warm, comforting brew also loosens mucus, making it easier to expel, which can help alleviate persistent coughing fits. Additionally, cinnamon's expectorant qualities help bring up phlegm from the lungs, allowing for a more productive and efficient clearing of the respiratory system.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Cinnamon's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause a cough, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Cinnamon's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde, help to reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, which can alleviate coughing and make breathing easier.
Expectorant properties: The expectorant properties of cinnamon tea help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the cinnamon plant contain a high concentration of coumarins, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as coughs and congestion.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the cinnamon plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an irritated throat and reduce coughing.
Bark: The bark of the cinnamon plant has natural antibacterial properties that can help combat infections that may contribute to persistent coughing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cinnamon bark or use high-quality dried cinnamon sticks for optimal flavor.
Combine one teaspoon of ground cinnamon with one cup of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for five to seven minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and aroma to release.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove loose particles.
Enjoy the warm, comforting tea hot, sweetened with honey if desired, in approximately ten minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Siberian ginseng teas helps with cough because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the throat and reduce congestion. The herbal tea also contains compounds that help to loosen mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing coughing fits. Additionally, Siberian ginseng is believed to have immunomodulatory effects, which can help to boost the body's defenses against infections that can cause coughs. Overall, Siberian ginseng teas provides a natural and effective way to alleviate cough symptoms and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Siberian Ginseng tea contains flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs caused by a cough.

Antiviral and antibacterial properties: The tea's active compounds, such as ginsenosides, exhibit antiviral and antibacterial activity, which can help combat viral and bacterial infections that cause coughs.

Expectorant properties: Siberian Ginseng tea has expectorant properties, meaning it can help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel and alleviate congestion caused by a cough.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in ginsenosides, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to soothe a dry or irritated cough.
Rhyzomes: Containing compounds that help to reduce inflammation and ease respiratory congestion, making it effective for relieving chesty coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried siberian ginseng roots in 20-30 grams for one day's supply.
Grind the dried roots into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Measure out two teaspoons of the powdered root and add to boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes before straining and serving hot or cold.
Drink the tea three times daily, ideally on an empty stomach or between meals.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum teas helps with cough because its unique blend of botanicals provides natural relief from congestion and irritation. The tea's key ingredient, eucalyptus oil, contains expectorants that help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the throat and bronchial passages, reducing coughing fits and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Eucalyptus oil: This property is known for its expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating congestion-related coughs.

Catechins: These bioflavonoids have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate coughing spasms, while also providing antioxidant protection against oxidative stress.

Volatile oils: Tasmanian Blue Gum tea's volatile oil content provides a soothing and calming effect on the mucous membranes, helping to quiet coughs and ease discomfort associated with upper respiratory tract infections.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the Tasmanian Blue Gum is rich in salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce inflammation and soothe coughs.
Roots: The roots of the plant contain saponins, which have natural expectorant properties that help to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 teaspoons of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves or 4-6 fresh leaves into a heat-resistant teapot.
Boil 1 cup of filtered water in a kettle and pour it over the eucalyptus globulus in the teapot.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-7 minutes, depending on desired strength and flavor.
Strain the tea leaves using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a large cup or teapot.
Serve hot, sweetened with honey if desired, and enjoy within 24 hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower teas helps with cough because its active compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, have been shown to have expectorant properties. These compounds help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, reducing congestion and relieving cough symptoms. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also soothe the throat and reduce irritation, making it an effective natural remedy for cough relief.
remedy_properties: Antitussive properties: The tea's active compounds, such as lactones and sesquiterpenes, help to reduce the urge to cough by soothing irritated mucous membranes in the throat and lungs.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory effects of Eastern Purple Coneflower tea can help to reduce swelling and irritation in the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and alleviating coughing spasms.

Antiviral and antibacterial properties: The tea's antiviral and antibacterial properties can help to combat infections that may be contributing to coughs, such as bronchitis or pneumonia, by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and viruses.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Eastern Purple Coneflower have been traditionally used to soothe coughs and relieve bronchial congestion due to its anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: The seeds of this plant contain mucilages, which help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, relieving coughs and sore throats.
remedy_recipe: Gather one cup of fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers for every 8 oz of water.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried echinacea purpurea flowers or two teaspoons of fresh flowers.
Heat filtered water in a tea kettle to a simmer for five to ten minutes.
Steep the echinacea flowers in hot water for seven to twelve minutes to allow flavors and nutrients to release.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and serve immediately.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat cough with herbal teas is by choosing teas with antitussive and expectorant properties. Thyme and licorice root teas are excellent choices for soothing irritated throats and reducing coughing. Peppermint tea, with its menthol content, helps relax the muscles of the respiratory tract, easing coughing spasms. Additionally, honey can be added to these teas for its soothing and cough-suppressing effects. Regularly drinking these herbal teas can provide relief and promote recovery from coughs. However, consult a healthcare professional if your cough persists for more than a week or is accompanied by other symptoms, such as fever or chest pain.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to cough that are treated with herbal teas include sore throat, bronchitis, and respiratory infections. Herbal teas like chamomile, licorice root, and ginger have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help alleviate irritation and reduce inflammation in the throat and respiratory tract. Peppermint tea contains menthol, which helps relax the muscles of the respiratory system and relieve congestion. Slippery elm bark tea coats and soothes irritated mucous membranes, providing relief from coughing and throat irritation. However, it is essential to consult a healthcare professional before starting any herbal remedy, especially for young children, pregnant women, and individuals with underlying health conditions.
